title: sdcTable
text: sdcTable provides algorithms protect primary sensitive table cells defined objects class sdcProblem. algorithms may selected shown : “GAUSS”: secondary suppression algorithm based Gaussian elimination implemented SSBtools::GaussSuppression(). “OPT”: protect complete hierarchical, multidimensional table . algorithm however suitable small problem instances. “HITAS”: solving secondary cell suppression problem applying cut branch algorithm sub-tables protected specific order “HYPERCUBE”: solving problem using heuristic based finding geometric hyper-cubes required protect primary sensitive table cells. algorithm extensively tested. better idea create batch-file usable tau-argus using createArgusInput() (?createArgusInput) solve problem using Argus. “SIMPLEHEURISTIC”: solving problem using heuristic approach aims protect exact recalculation values. Internally, attacker-problems iteratively solved primary sensitive cells protected. Note Performance: Starting version > 0.33, attack() protectTable(..., method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC") support optional n_workers parameter. n_workers > 1, underlying attacker-problems solved parallel using future.apply framework, significantly reducing computation time large tables. parameter passed protectTable() directly underlying attack() calls; otherwise, processing performed sequentially. Warning: Parallel processing spawns multiple independent R sessions, requiring memory allocation. Depending size constraint matrix complexity optimization, can lead significant increase RAM usage. Ensure system sufficient memory available scaling n_workers large table structures. “SIMPLEHEURISTIC_OLD”: heuristic procedure implemented sdcTable versions < 0.32. show protect data using available algorithms. extensive discussion possible parameters look manual help page function protectTable(). look resulting objects can observe number secondary suppressions required protect 15 primary sensitive cells (default exact re-calculation given sliding protection levels 1 primary sensitive cell) differs. Using “OPT”-algorithm, total 22 cells marked secondary suppressions. using “HITAS”-algorithm, required additionally suppress 13 cells. total 27 cells selected marked secondary suppressions “HYPERCUBE” algorithm used 27 additional suppressions required fast heuristic simple procedure “SIMPLEHEURISTIC” 23 supps Gaussian elimination method. One now easily get information resulting output objects instances class safeObj using function getInfo() applying summary-method. former show extract final data set can achieved follows: can see final result data set contains columns specified input data set along another column sdcStatus specifies anonymization state table cell. latter show apply summary method. can done applying following code: see summary provides kind useful information algorithm used protect primary sensitive cells, time taken solve problem, number primary sensitive secondary suppressed cells well number cells may published. Also, excerpt final data set shown. also like mention iterative algorithm available function protectLinkedTables() allows protect two tables common table cells. function takes two objects class sdcProblem input list defining common cells tables. Details construct list-element given manual help-page protectLinkedTables().
code: resGAUSS <- protectTable(prob.completeDat, method = "GAUSS") resHITAS <- protectTable(prob.completeDat, method = "HITAS") resOPT <- protectTable(prob.completeDat, method = "OPT") resHYPER <- protectTable(prob.completeDat, method = "HYPERCUBE") resSIMPLE <- protectTable(prob.completeDat, method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC") finalData <- getInfo(resOPT, type = "finalData") print(head(finalData)) ##        V1     V2     V3  Freq   numVal1   numVal2 sdcStatus ##    <char> <char> <char> <num>     <num>     <num>    <char> ## 1:    Tot    Tot    Tot  1000 294693.72 298707.90         s ## 2:    Tot    Tot      a   178  49115.62  49784.65         s ## 3:    Tot    Tot      b   159  49115.62  49784.65         s ## 4:    Tot    Tot      c   177  49115.62  49784.65         s ## 5:    Tot    Tot      d   144  49115.62  49784.65         s ## 6:    Tot    Tot      e   150  49115.62  49784.65         s summary(resOPT) ##  ## ##################################### ## ### Summary of the protected data ### ## ##################################### ## --> The input data have been protected using algorithm 'OPT' ## --> To protect 15 primary sensitive cells, 22 cells were additionally suppressed ## --> A total of 131 cells may be published ##  ## ################################### ## ### Structure of protected data ### ## ################################### ## Classes 'safeObj', 'data.table' and 'data.frame':    168 obs. of  7 variables: ##  $ V1       : chr  "Tot" "Tot" "Tot" "Tot" ... ##  $ V2       : chr  "Tot" "Tot" "Tot" "Tot" ... ##  $ V3       : chr  "Tot" "a" "b" "c" ... ##  $ Freq     : num  1000 178 159 177 144 150 192 487 82 80 ... ##  $ numVal1  : num  294694 49116 49116 49116 49116 ... ##  $ numVal2  : num  298708 49785 49785 49785 49785 ... ##  $ sdcStatus: chr  "s" "s" "s" "s" ... ##  - attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<pointer: 0x55cf48c36ef0>  ## NULL

title: Create input files for tauArgus — createArgusInput
text: obj object class sdcProblem sdcTable typ (character) either "microdata" "tabular" verbose (logical) TRUE, contents batch-file written prompt path path, (temporary) files written (amongst batch-files). file written folder belonging problem contains random id filename. solver solver used. allowed choices "FREE" "CPLEX" "XPRESS" case "CPLEX" used, also mandatory specify argument licensefile needs absolute path cplex license file method secondary cell suppression algorithm, possible choices include: "MOD": modular approach. specified, following arguments ... can additionally set: MaxTimePerSubtable: number specifiying max. time (minutes) spent subtable SingleSingle: 0/1 (default=1) SingleMultiple: 0/1 (default=1) MinFreq: 0/1 (default=1) "GH": hypercube. specified, following arguments ... can additionally set: BoundPercentage: Default percentage proctect primary suppressed cells, default 75 ModelSize: dealing small (0) large (1) model? (default=1) ApplySingleton: singletons additionally protected? 0/1 (default=1) "OPT": optimal cell suppression. specified, following arguments ... can additionally set: MaxComputingTime: number specifiying max. allowed computing time (minutes) primSuppRules rules primary suppression, provided list. details, please look examples . responsevar variable tabulated (defaults frequencies). details see tau-argus manual section 4.4.4. shadowvar specified, variable used apply safety rules, defaults responsevar. details see tau-argus manual section 4.4.4. costvar specified, variable describes costs suppressing individual cell. details see tau-argus manual section 4.4.4. requestvar specified, variable (0/1-coded) contains information records request protection. Records 1 protected case corresponding request rule matches. ignored, tabular input used. holdingvar specified, variable contains information records grouped together. ignored, tabular input used. ... allows specify additional parameters selected suppression-method described well licensefile clase "CPLEX" specified argument solver.

title: Create input files for tauArgus — createArgusInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # loading micro data from sdcTable utils::data("microdata1", package="sdcTable") microdata1$num1 <- rnorm(mean = 100, sd = 25, nrow(microdata1)) microdata1$num2 <- round(rnorm(mean = 500, sd=125, nrow(microdata1)),2) microdata1$weight <- sample(10:100, nrow(microdata1), replace = TRUE)  dim_region <- hier_create(root = "Total", nodes = LETTERS[1:4])  dim_region_dupl <- hier_create(root = "Total", nodes = LETTERS[1:4]) dim_region_dupl <- hier_add(dim_region_dupl, root = "B", nodes = c("b1")) dim_region_dupl <- hier_add(dim_region_dupl, root = "D", nodes = c("d1"))  dim_gender <- hier_create(root = "Total", nodes = c("male", "female"))  dimList <- list(region = dim_region, gender = dim_gender) dimList_dupl  <- list(region = dim_region_dupl, gender = dim_gender) dimVarInd <- 1:2 numVarInd <- 3:5 sampWeightInd <- 6  # creating an object of class \code{\link{sdcProblem-class}} obj <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList,   dimVarInd = dimVarInd,   numVarInd = numVarInd,   sampWeightInd = sampWeightInd)  # creating an object of class \code{\link{sdcProblem-class}} containing "duplicated" codes obj_dupl <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList_dupl,   dimVarInd = dimVarInd,   numVarInd = numVarInd,   sampWeightInd = sampWeightInd)  ## create primary suppression rules primSuppRules <- list() primSuppRules[[1]] <- list(type = "freq", n = 5, rg = 20) primSuppRules[[2]] <- list(type = "p", n = 5, p = 20) # other supported formats are: # list(type = "nk", n=5, k=20) # list(type = "zero", rg = 5) # list(type = "mis", val = 1) # list(type = "wgt", val = 1) # list(type = "man", val = 20)  ## create batchInput object bO_md1 <- createArgusInput(   obj = obj,   typ = "microdata",   path = tempdir(),   solver = "FREE",   method = "OPT",   primSuppRules = primSuppRules,   responsevar = "num1")  bO_td1 <- createArgusInput(   obj = obj,   typ = "tabular",   path = tempdir(),   solver = "FREE",   method = "OPT")  bO_td2 <- createArgusInput(   obj = obj_dupl,   typ = "tabular",   path = tempdir(),   solver = "FREE",   method = "OPT")  ## in case CPLEX should be used, it is required to specify argument licensefile bO_md2 <- createArgusInput(   obj = obj,   typ = "microdata",   path = tempdir(),   solver = "CPLEX",   method = "OPT",   primSuppRules = primSuppRules,   responsevar = "num1",   licensefile = "/path/to/my/cplexlicense") } # }

title: Create input for RegSDC/other Tools — createRegSDCInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ utils::data("microdata1", package = "sdcTable") head(microdata1)  # define the problem dim_region <- hier_create(root = "total", nodes = sort(unique(microdata1$region))) dim_gender <- hier_create(root = "total", nodes = sort(unique(microdata1$gender)))  prob <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = list(region = dim_region, gender = dim_gender),   freqVarInd = NULL )  # suppress some cells prob <- primarySuppression(prob, type = "freq", maxN = 15)  # compute input for RegSDC-package inp_regsdc <- createRegSDCInput(x = prob, chk = TRUE)  # estimate innner cells based on linear dependencies res_regsdc <- RegSDC::SuppressDec(   x = as.matrix(inp_regsdc$x),   z = inp_regsdc$z_supp,   y = inp_regsdc$y)[, 1]  # check if inner cells are all protected df <- data.frame(   freqs_orig = inp_regsdc$z[inp_regsdc$info$is_innercell == TRUE, ],   freqs_supp = inp_regsdc$z_supp[inp_regsdc$info$is_innercell == TRUE, ],   regsdc = res_regsdc )  subset(df, df$regsdc == df$freqs_orig & is.na(freqs_supp))  } # }

title: Create a problem instance — makeProblem

title: Create a problem instance — makeProblem
code: # loading micro data utils::data("microdata1", package = "sdcTable")  # we can observe that we have a micro data set consisting # of two spanning variables ('region' and 'gender') and one # numeric variable ('val')  # specify structure of hierarchical variable 'region' # levels 'A' to 'D' sum up to a Total dim.region <- data.frame(  levels=c('@','@@','@@','@@','@@'),  codes=c('Total', 'A','B','C','D'),  stringsAsFactors=FALSE)  # specify structure of hierarchical variable 'gender' # using create_node() and add_nodes() (see ?manage_hierarchies) dim.gender <- hier_create(root = "Total", nodes = c("male", "female")) hier_display(dim.gender) #> Total #> ├─male #> └─female  # create a named list with each element being a data-frame # containing information on one dimensional variable and # the names referring to variables in the input data dimList <- list(region = dim.region, gender = dim.gender)  # third column containts a numeric variable numVarInd <- 3  # no variables holding counts, numeric values, weights or sampling # weights are available in the input data # creating an problem instance using numeric indices p1 <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList,   numVarInd = 3 # third variable in `data` )  # using variable names is also possible p2 <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList,   numVarInd = "val" )  # what do we have? print(class(p1)) #> [1] "sdcProblem" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "sdcTable"  # have a look at the data df1 <- sdcProb2df(p1, addDups = TRUE,   addNumVars = TRUE, dimCodes = "original") df2 <- sdcProb2df(p2, addDups=TRUE,   addNumVars = TRUE, dimCodes = "original") print(df1) #> Key: <strID> #>      strID  freq sdcStatus   val region gender #>     <char> <num>    <char> <num> <char> <char> #>  1:   0000   100         s  1284  Total  Total #>  2:   0001    55         s   802  Total   male #>  3:   0002    45         s   482  Total female #>  4:   0100    20         s   198      A  Total #>  5:   0101    18         s   178      A   male #>  6:   0102     2         s    20      A female #>  7:   0200    33         s   344      B  Total #>  8:   0201    14         s   140      B   male #>  9:   0202    19         s   204      B female #> 10:   0300    22         s   224      C  Total #> 11:   0301    12         s   118      C   male #> 12:   0302    10         s   106      C female #> 13:   0400    25         s   518      D  Total #> 14:   0401    11         s   366      D   male #> 15:   0402    14         s   152      D female  identical(df1, df2) #> [1] TRUE

title: Apply primary suppression — primarySuppression
text: object sdcProblem object. type character string defining primary suppression rule. Allowed values : "freq": apply frequency rule (uses maxN allowZeros). "nk": apply nk-dominance rule (uses n k). "p": apply p-percent rule (uses p). "pq": apply pq-rule (uses p q). ... parameters selected primary suppression rule: maxN: scalar integerish; cells counts <= maxN suppressed. Defaults 3. allowZeros: scalar logical; TRUE, empty cells (frequency 0) considered sensitive. Defaults FALSE. Empty cells flagged sensitive marked z (published, used secondary suppression). p: scalar integerish; threshold parameter p-percent pq-rules. Defaults 80. q: scalar numeric; threshold parameter pq-rule. Defaults 50. n: scalar integerish; parameter n nk-dominance rule. Defaults 2. k: scalar integerish; parameter k nk-dominance rule. Defaults 85. numVarName: scalar character; name numerical variable used dominance-based rules. mandatory nk, p, pq rules.

title: Protecting sdcProblem objects — protectTable
text: object sdcProblem object created using makeProblem() modified primarySuppression() method character vector length 1 specifying algorithm used protect primary sensitive table cells. Allowed values : "OPT": protect complete problem using cut branch algorithm. optimal algorithm used small problem-instances . "HITAS": split overall problem smaller problems. problems protected using top-approach. "HYPERCUBE": protect complete problem protecting sub-tables fast heuristic based finding suppressing geometric structures (n-dimensional cubes) required protect primary sensitive table cells. "SIMPLEHEURISTIC" "SIMPLEHEURISTIC_OLD": heuristic procedures might applied large(r) problem instances; "SIMPLEHEURISTIC" based constraints; also solves attacker problems make sure primary sensitive cell recomputed; "SIMPLEHEURISTIC_OLD" implementation sdcTable versions prior 0.32; implementation possibly unsafe fast; advised check results using attack() afterwards. ... parameters used protection algorithm selected. Parameters can changed : general parameters: verbose: logical scalar (default FALSE) defining verbose output produced save: logical scalar defining temporary results saved current working directory (TRUE) (FALSE) default value. parameters used "HITAS" "OPT" algorithms: solver: character vector length 1 defining solver used. Currently available choices limited "highs". timeLimit: numeric vector length 1 (NULL) defining time limit minutes cut branch algorithm stop return possible non-optimal solution. Parameter safe default value NULL maxVars: integerish number (NULL) defining maximum problem size terms decision variables optimization tried. number decision variables current problem larger parameter maxVars, possible non-optimal, heuristic solution calculated. Parameter maxVars default value NULL (restrictions) fastSolution: logical scalar defining (default FALSE) cut branch algorithm started possibly non-optimal heuristic solution returned independent parameter maxVars. fixVariables: logical scalar (default TRUE) defining whether tried fix variables 0 1 based reduced costs early cut branch algorithm. approxPerc: integerish scalar defines percentage integer solution cut branch algorithm accepted optimal respect upper bound given (relaxed) solution master problem. default value set 10 useC: logical scalar defining c++ implementation secondary cell suppression problem used, defaults FALSE parameters used "HYPERCUBE" procedure: protectionLevel: numeric vector length 1 specifying required protectionlevel procedure. default value 80 suppMethod: character vector length 1 defining rule select 'optimal' cube protect single sensitive cells. Possible choices : minSupps: minimize number additional secondary suppressions (also default setting). minSum: minimize sum counts additional suppressed cells minSumLogs: minimize log sum additional suppressed cells suppAdditionalQuader: logical vector length 1 specfifying additional cubes suppressed secondary suppressions 'optimal' cube 'singletons'. Parameter suppAdditionalQuader default value FALSE parameter(s) used protect_linked_tables(): maxIter: integerish number specifying maximal number interations make trying protect common cells two different tables. default value parameter 10 parameters used "SIMPLEHEURISTIC" "SIMPLEHEURISTIC_OLD" procedure: detectSingletons: logical, singleton-detection procedure run protecting data, defaults FALSE. threshold: NULL (default) integerish number (> 0). specified, procedure similar singleton-detection procedure run makes sure (simple) rows table instance contains primary sensitive cells suppressed number contributors >= specified threshold. n_workers: (integer >= 1, method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC" ); number parallel workers. Defaults 1 (sequential). > 1, parallel execution via future.apply used accelerate processing. Warning: Higher values increase RAM usage. attack_threshold: (numeric >= 0, method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC" ); safety threshold used determine primary sensitive cell considered protected. cell deemed "safe" difference computed upper lower bound (abs(upper - lower)) strictly greater threshold. Defaults 1e-8. parameters used "GAUSS" procedure; details please see ?SSBtools::GaussSuppression default values function: removeDuplicated: duplicated columns removed running protection algorithm whenEmptySuppressed: function called primary suppressed input problematic; NULL (default) apply function whenEmptyUnsuppressed: function called empty candidate cells aredevto problematic; NULL (default) apply function singletonMethod: parameter singletonMethod SSBtools::GaussSuppression(); default "anySum"

title: Protect two tables with common cells — protectLinkedTables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load micro data for further processing utils::data("microdata2", package = "sdcTable")  # table1: defined by variables 'gender' and 'ecoOld' md1 <- microdata2[,c(2,3,5)]  # table2: defined by variables 'region', 'gender' and 'ecoNew' md2 <- microdata2[,c(1,2,4,5)]  # we need to create information on the hierarchies # variable 'region': exists only in md2 d_region <- hier_create(root = "Tot", nodes = c("R1", "R2"))  # variable 'gender': exists in both datasets d_gender <- hier_create(root = "Tot", nodes = c("m", "f"))  # variable 'eco1': exists only in md1 d_eco1 <- hier_create(root = "Tot", nodes = c("A", "B")) d_eco1 <- hier_add(d_eco1, root = "A", nodes = c("Aa", "Ab")) d_eco1 <- hier_add(d_eco1, root = "B", nodes = c("Ba", "Bb"))  # variable 'ecoNew': exists only in md2 d_eco2 <- hier_create(root = "Tot", nodes = c("C", "D")) d_eco2 <- hier_add(d_eco2, root = "C", nodes = c("Ca", "Cb", "Cc")) d_eco2 <- hier_add(d_eco2, root = "D", nodes = c("Da", "Db", "Dc"))  # creating objects holding information on dimensions dl1 <- list(gender = d_gender, ecoOld = d_eco1) dl2 <- list(region = d_region, gender = d_gender, ecoNew = d_eco2)  # creating input objects for further processing. # For details, see ?makeProblem. p1 <- makeProblem(   data = md1,   dimList = dl1,   dimVarInd = 1:2,   numVarInd = 3)  p2 <- makeProblem(   data = md2,   dimList = dl2,   dimVarInd = 1:3,   numVarInd = 4)  # the cell specified by gender == "Tot" and ecoOld == "A" # is one of the common cells! -> we mark it as primary suppression p1 <- change_cellstatus(   object = p1,   specs = data.frame(gender = "Tot", ecoOld = "A"),   rule = "u",   verbose = FALSE)  # the cell specified by region == "Tot" and gender == "f" and ecoNew == "C" # is one of the common cells! -> we mark it as primary suppression p2 <- change_cellstatus(   object = p2,   specs = data.frame(region = "Tot", gender = "f", ecoNew = "C"),   rule = "u",   verbose = FALSE)  # specifying input to define common cells common_cells <- list()  # variable "gender" common_cells$v.gender <- list() common_cells$v.gender[[1]] <- "gender" # variable name in "p1" common_cells$v.gender[[2]] <- "gender" # variable name in "p2"  # "gender" has equal characteristics on both datasets -> keyword "ALL" common_cells$v.gender[[3]] <- "ALL"  # variables: "ecoOld" and "ecoNew" common_cells$v.eco <- list() common_cells$v.eco[[1]] <- "ecoOld" # variable name in "p1" common_cells$v.eco[[2]] <- "ecoNew" # variable name in "p2"  # vector of common characteristics: # "A" and "B" in variable "ecoOld" in "p1" common_cells$v.eco[[3]] <- c("A", "B")  # correspond to codes "C" and "D" in variable "ecoNew" in "p2" common_cells$v.eco[[4]] <- c("C", "D")  # protect the linked data result <- protect_linked_tables(   x = p1,   y = p2,   common_cells = common_cells,   verbose = TRUE)  # having a look at the results result_tab1 <- result$x result_tab2 <- result$y summary(result_tab1) summary(result_tab2) } # }

code: # loading micro data utils::data("microdata1", package = "sdcTable")  # we can observe that we have a micro data set consisting # of two spanning variables ('region' and 'gender') and one # numeric variable ('val')  # specify structure of hierarchical variable 'region' # levels 'A' to 'D' sum up to a Total dim.region <- data.frame(  levels=c('@','@@','@@','@@','@@'),  codes=c('Total', 'A','B','C','D'),  stringsAsFactors=FALSE)  # specify structure of hierarchical variable 'gender' # using create_node() and add_nodes() (see ?manage_hierarchies) dim.gender <- hier_create(root = "Total", nodes = c("male", "female")) hier_display(dim.gender) #> Total #> ├─male #> └─female  # create a named list with each element being a data-frame # containing information on one dimensional variable and # the names referring to variables in the input data dimList <- list(region = dim.region, gender = dim.gender)  # third column containts a numeric variable numVarInd <- 3  # no variables holding counts, numeric values, weights or sampling # weights are available in the input data # creating an problem instance using numeric indices p1 <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList,   numVarInd = 3 # third variable in `data` )  # using variable names is also possible p2 <- makeProblem(   data = microdata1,   dimList = dimList,   numVarInd = "val" )  # what do we have? print(class(p1)) #> [1] "sdcProblem" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "sdcTable"  # have a look at the data df1 <- sdcProb2df(p1, addDups = TRUE,   addNumVars = TRUE, dimCodes = "original") df2 <- sdcProb2df(p2, addDups=TRUE,   addNumVars = TRUE, dimCodes = "original") print(df1) #> Key: <strID> #>      strID  freq sdcStatus   val region gender #>     <char> <num>    <char> <num> <char> <char> #>  1:   0000   100         s  1284  Total  Total #>  2:   0001    55         s   802  Total   male #>  3:   0002    45         s   482  Total female #>  4:   0100    20         s   198      A  Total #>  5:   0101    18         s   178      A   male #>  6:   0102     2         s    20      A female #>  7:   0200    33         s   344      B  Total #>  8:   0201    14         s   140      B   male #>  9:   0202    19         s   204      B female #> 10:   0300    22         s   224      C  Total #> 11:   0301    12         s   118      C   male #> 12:   0302    10         s   106      C female #> 13:   0400    25         s   518      D  Total #> 14:   0401    11         s   366      D   male #> 15:   0402    14         s   152      D female  identical(df1, df2) #> [1] TRUE

title: Set/Update information in sdcProblem or problemInstance objects — setInfo
text: object class sdcProblem created, every cell assigned internal anonymization state. codes track lifecycle cell throughout suppression process: "s" ("Publishable"): cell safe can published (default). "z"("Forced Publishable"): cell must suppressed circumstances. "u" (*"Primary Suppressed"): cell identified sensitive requires protection (typically assigned via primarySuppression()). "x" ("Secondary Suppressed"): cell suppressed protect primary sensitive cells (typically assigned via protectTable()). "w" ("Extension Cell"): cell exists object never published. treated suppressed cell internal algorithms. useful known advance specific codes hierarchy excluded publication. Initially makeProblem(), cells coded "s" "z". status transitions "u" "x" suppression algorithms applied. setInfo() change_cellstatus() allow manual state modifications, primarySuppression() automatically identifies primary sensitive cells assigns state "u". protectTable(), additional cells identified set "x" ensure primary sensitive cells adequately protected.

code: # load example-problem with suppressions # (same as example from ?primarySuppression) p <- sdc_testproblem(with_supps = TRUE)  # which is the overall total? idx <- which.max(getInfo(p, "freq")); idx #> [1] 1  # we see that the cell with idx = 1 is the overall total and its # anonymization state of the total can be extracted as follows: print(getInfo(p, type = "sdcStatus")[idx]) #> [1] "s"  # we want this cell to never be suppressed p <- setInfo(p, type = "sdcStatus", index = idx, input = "z")  # we can verify this: print(getInfo(p, type = "sdcStatus")[idx]) #> [1] "z"  # changing slot 'UPL' for all cells inp <- data.frame(   strID = getInfo(p, "strID"),   UPL_old = getInfo(p, "UPL") ) inp$UPL_new <- inp$UPL_old + 1 p <- setInfo(p, type = "UPL", index = 1:nrow(inp), input = inp$UPL_new)

text: attack() now features n_workers argument, enabling parallel execution attacker problems via future.apply package (available). function now exposes n_workers using method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC", allowing parallelized internal attacker calls (passed attack()). Added argument attack_threshold method = "SIMPLEHEURISTIC". allows users define safety tolerance (abs(upper - lower) > attack_threshold) directly protecting tables. Documentation updates: Added detailed explanations performance warnings regarding RAM usage parallel processing package vignette function man-pages. Added threshold argument attack() flexible safety checks (defaults 1e-8). Optimized internal create_m_matrix() faster matrix construction lower memory overhead. Refactored/Simplified internal parameter-generation Added centralized Cell Status Codes section sdcProblem documentation, explaining states like "z" (empty cells) "w". section now reused across functions modify cell statuses (UserIssue #319) Significantly optimized contributing_indices() regarding running-time memory usage
